signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Shock + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.28 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite Iran war escalation and Strait of Hormuz naval blockade. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) remains crowded; carry dominates while VIX remains at 18.4 (normal volatility). Intervention risk rises on a test of 160.00 as energy supply deficits (8-10m barrels) support USD via terms-of-trade advantage.

signals.title: USD/NZD — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Divergence
signals.text: USD/NZD G8 spread at 0.979% confirms a bilateral theme favoring USD. NZD COT LF net short -16,833 (23.0% long) vs RBNZ hike bias creates a short-squeeze risk, however, RBNZ's Savage is pushing back on supply-shock hikes. 150bp USD carry advantage and energy-driven USD demand keep NZD/USD under pressure at 0.5863.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7156 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) vs retail 86% short. Institutional vs retail divergence is at an extreme; retail is fading the institutional long. While 35bp AUD carry premium supports the pair, retail capitulation risk rises if price extends higher. RBA hold bias limits immediate upside momentum.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Positioning
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.725 (z=+1.89σ) — historical norm of -0.142 is broken. EUR/USD at 1.1688 is decoupling from equity weakness as market focuses on the 175bp Fed-ECB carry disadvantage. EUR COT LF net short -18,293 (45.4% long) indicates neutral-to-bearish institutional sentiment as the pair tracks DXY strength.
